title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Rising to the Occasion: The Artisan Bread Revolution
content.text: At five o'clock every morning, before most of the city is awake, Elena Marsh is already at work in her bakery, shaping loaves by hand. She is one of thousands of artisan bakers who have helped trigger a remarkable revival of traditional bread-making over the past two decades. (37) [37] Este renacimiento surgió como reacción a la industria. [/37] Their movement grew, in large part, as a reaction against the industrially produced loaves that had dominated supermarket shelves since the 1960s. || Industrial bread is made quickly. Mechanical processes can turn raw flour into a sliced loaf in under two hours using a combination of additives, enzymes and high-speed mixing. (38) [38] El pan artesanal requiere un proceso radicalmente distinto. [/38] Artisan bread, by contrast, relies on long fermentation — sometimes lasting twenty-four hours or more — which develops flavour and improves digestibility. Many artisan bakers use only flour, water, salt and a natural sourdough starter. || The sourdough starter is central to the craft. It is a living culture of wild yeast and bacteria, fed daily with fresh flour and water. (39) [39] Algunos panaderos mantienen sus masas madre durante décadas. [/39] Some bakers speak of their starters almost as living companions, giving them names and taking them on holiday to avoid disruption to the baking routine. || Learning to bake well takes considerable time. Marsh spent three years working in bakeries in France and Germany before she felt confident enough to open her own business. (40) [40] La formación en el extranjero es habitual entre los panaderos artesanos. [/40] 'You have to learn to read the dough,' she explains. 'Temperature, humidity, the age of your flour — all of these things affect the final loaf.' || The artisan bread movement has also inspired a surge in home baking. During the pandemic lockdowns of 2020, sourdough starter recipes spread rapidly across social media. (41) [41] Las redes sociales amplificaron el interés por la panificación casera. [/41] Flour became temporarily unavailable in many countries as millions of people attempted their first loaves. || Today, artisan bakeries are thriving in cities and market towns alike. Customers willingly pay a premium for bread that takes days rather than hours to produce. (42) [42] El precio más alto refleja el valor del tiempo y el conocimiento invertidos. [/42] For bakers like Marsh, that premium represents not just the cost of ingredients, but the value of a craft passed down through generations.

paragraphs.A: Many have trained abroad, absorbing techniques that simply are not taught in domestic culinary schools.
paragraphs.B: Loyal customers argue that bread made this way is worth every extra penny.
paragraphs.C: Some starters in professional bakeries have been kept alive and active for well over fifty years.
paragraphs.D: The process could hardly be more different from the factory methods it seeks to replace.
paragraphs.E: Videos of shaping and scoring techniques attracted millions of viewers almost overnight.
paragraphs.F: For many consumers, buying artisan bread became an act of deliberate resistance to mass production.
paragraphs.G: This communal enthusiasm transformed bread-making into one of the defining domestic trends of the decade.

questions.explanation: F introduce la motivación del movimiento artesano como reacción consciente a la producción industrial.
D contrasta directamente el proceso industrial descrito antes con el método artesano que se explica a continuación.
C da cifras concretas sobre la longevidad de las masas madre antes de la anécdota sobre bautizarlas.
A generaliza la experiencia de Marsh en el extranjero, indicando que es una práctica habitual en el sector.
E explica cómo las redes sociales difundieron el interés por el pan en casa, enlazando con la escasez de harina.
B refuerza la disposición de los clientes a pagar más, anticipando la reflexión final de Marsh sobre el valor del oficio.
